east-village.note: Walk to Essex St for the J/Z straight to Sutphin Blvd, then the $8.75 AirTrain; slow but only one transfer — a car runs ~$70-100.

lower-east-side.note: One of the easier downtown JFK rides: J/Z from Delancey-Essex runs direct to Sutphin Blvd-Archer Av for the AirTrain.

williamsburg.note: L to Broadway Junction, A to Howard Beach, AirTrain — or the slow one-seat J from Marcy Av to Sutphin; budget ~80 min either way, or a ~$55-75 car.

east-williamsburg.note: L to Broadway Junction, A to Howard Beach, then the AirTrain; tolerable off-peak, miserable with luggage at rush — a car is ~$55-70.

greenpoint.note: The worst of the ten: G to Court Sq, E to Jamaica, AirTrain — call it 90 minutes on a good day; most people just book a ~$60-80 car.

bushwick.note: Best-placed of the Brooklyn picks: L to Broadway Junction then A to Howard Beach, or J/Z along Broadway straight to Sutphin; ~$50-65 by car.

astoria.note: Awkward Queens-to-Queens trip: N/W to the 7 to the E at Roosevelt Av, then AirTrain — roughly 90 minutes, so most people pay ~$50-70 for a car.

long-island-city.note: Surprisingly clean: one E train from Court Sq to Sutphin Blvd-Archer Av, then the $8.75 AirTrain.

hells-kitchen.note: Fastest of the ten: LIRR from Moynihan/Penn to Jamaica in ~20 minutes plus AirTrain, or the cheaper one-seat E; a cab is $90-120 with the $70 flat fare, tolls, and fees.

yorkville.note: 4/5/6 to Grand Central, LIRR to Jamaica in ~20 minutes, then AirTrain — long but smooth; a cab runs $90-120 either direction.

harlem.note: A genuine one-seat ride: the A from 125 St runs all the way to Howard Beach (~60 minutes on the train), then the $8.75 AirTrain — long but simple; a cab is $90-120 with the flat fare and tolls.

ridgewood.note: M to Myrtle Av then the J/Z to Sutphin Blvd for the AirTrain, or L from Halsey to Broadway Junction and the A to Howard Beach; clunky but workable — a car runs ~$50-65.

crown-heights.note: One of the best JFK setups in the city: the A from Nostrand or Utica runs express to Howard Beach in ~25 minutes, then the AirTrain; from the Eastern Parkway side add a walk or bus up to Fulton St. Cars run ~$45-60.

prospect-heights.note: Walk to Atlantic Terminal at Barclays, LIRR to Jamaica in ~20 minutes, then the AirTrain — rivals Midtown for the fastest JFK trip; a car runs ~$50-65.

fort-greene.note: Atlantic Terminal is a short walk: LIRR to Jamaica in ~20 minutes plus the $8.75 AirTrain; from Clinton Hill the C to Euclid Av with an A transfer also works — a car is ~$50-65.

bed-stuy.note: Quietly excellent: the A from Nostrand or Utica runs express to Howard Beach for the AirTrain, and the J/Z from the Broadway side goes straight to Sutphin Blvd; a car is ~$45-60.

chelsea.note: One-seat E from 23 St-8th Av to Sutphin Blvd-Archer Av then the AirTrain, or the A from 14 St to Howard Beach; a cab is $90-120 with the $70 flat fare, tolls, and fees.

west-village.note: Better than most of Manhattan: the A from W 4 St runs express to Howard Beach in ~42 minutes, then the AirTrain; the one-seat E to Sutphin is the backup, and cabs run $90-120.

east-village.note: F or M out to Jackson Heights-Roosevelt Av, then the free Q70 to the terminals; a car is ~$40-60.

lower-east-side.note: F from Delancey or East Broadway to Roosevelt Av, then the free Q70; workable, or a ~$40-60 car.

williamsburg.note: No good rail link from north Brooklyn: G to Court Sq, 7 to 74 St, free Q70 — most people pay $35-60 for a car instead.

east-williamsburg.note: A three-seat slog (L, G, 7, then the Q70) for a trip of about six miles; with a flight bag a ~$35-55 car wins.

greenpoint.note: LGA is only ~5 miles away but transit is a G-to-7-to-Q70 chain; a ~$30-45 car is hard to resist.

bushwick.note: The one-seat M loops through Manhattan before reaching Roosevelt Av for the Q70 — about 80 minutes for a 7-mile trip; cars run ~$40-55.

astoria.note: The easy one: the 24-hour M60 SBS runs down Astoria Blvd straight into the terminals — 25-40 minutes door to door, no reason for a cab.

long-island-city.note: 7 (or E/M from Court Sq) to 74 St-Roosevelt Av for the free Q70; ~45 minutes, or a ~$30-40 car.

hells-kitchen.note: E or 7 out to 74 St-Roosevelt Av, then the free Q70; cabs from Midtown run $50-75 with tolls and can crawl in traffic.

yorkville.note: 4/5/6 one stop to 125 St, then the M60 SBS over the RFK Bridge; 125th St traffic can drag, and a car is ~$35-50.

harlem.note: Harlem's ace: the 24-hour M60 SBS picks up along 125th St and runs over the RFK Bridge straight into the terminals — 25-40 minutes, no reason for a cab.

ridgewood.note: Brutal for a ~6-mile trip: the one-seat M from Fresh Pond or Forest Av loops through Manhattan before reaching Roosevelt Av for the free Q70 — call it 90 minutes; a ~$35-50 car is the real answer.

crown-heights.note: No good rail link: the least-bad route is the 4/5 express from Franklin Av to 125 St, then the M60 SBS over the RFK — most people just book a ~$40-60 car.

prospect-heights.note: Nothing direct — 2/3 from Grand Army Plaza or Barclays into Manhattan, the 7 out to 74 St-Roosevelt Av, then the free Q70; with luggage a ~$40-55 car wins.

fort-greene.note: G from Clinton-Washington or Fulton St up to Court Sq, the 7 to 74 St-Roosevelt Av, then the free Q70 — a three-seat chain most people skip for a ~$40-55 car.

bed-stuy.note: Rough: the G from Bedford-Nostrand to Court Sq then the 7 and Q70, or the one-seat M from Myrtle Av that loops through Manhattan first — ~80 minutes for ~7 miles, so budget a ~$40-55 car.

chelsea.note: E from 23 St-8th Av (or the 7 from Hudson Yards) out to 74 St-Roosevelt Av, then the free Q70; cabs run $45-65 with tolls and can crawl on the BQE approach.

west-village.note: E or M from W 4 St straight out to Roosevelt Av for the free Q70 — tedious but a single transfer; a car is ~$45-65 depending on Midtown traffic.

east-village.note: N/Q/R/W from Union Sq or 8 St run to 57 St-7 Av, then you walk in at 59 St; the 6 to 68 St-Hunter College covers the Fifth Ave entrances.

lower-east-side.note: The weekday B from Grand St lands at the 72 St-Central Park West entrance, no transfer; the D gets you Columbus Circle, and from the Essex end the F to 57 St leaves a walk.

williamsburg.note: No one-seat ride: L to Union Sq then a N/Q/R/W up Broadway, or M to 34 St then B/D up Central Park West — the park is a planned trip, not a stroll.

east-williamsburg.note: Same L-plus-Midtown-transfer as Williamsburg with ten more minutes on the front; for casual green space you'll use McCarren or Cooper Park instead.

greenpoint.note: G to Court Sq, E to 7 Av-53 St, then a walk up Seventh into the park — nothing about this trip is quick, so weekend visits take commitment.

bushwick.note: M up Sixth Ave then B/D to Central Park West, or L to Union Sq then a Broadway train — call it an hour to the lawn, the worst of the ten.

astoria.note: N/W run straight to 57 St-7 Av two blocks from the park's south edge — no transfer, then a ten-minute walk up to the lawn.

long-island-city.note: E or M from Court Sq to the 53 St stops then a walk up; the F from 21 St-Queensbridge reaches 57 St two blocks from the park. Short rides, walk-heavy endings.

hells-kitchen.note: From the upper 40s and 50s you can simply walk to Columbus Circle; otherwise the 1 to 66 St or any A/C/B/D to 59 St puts you at the park edge in minutes.

yorkville.note: Q one stop to 72 St then a crosstown walk to the Fifth Ave entrance — or skip the train entirely, since much of Yorkville can walk into the park along Museum Mile.

harlem.note: The park is effectively your backyard: Central Park North sits at 110th St, a 10-15 minute walk from much of Central Harlem, or two quick 2/3 stops from 125 St-Lenox to the corner entrance.

ridgewood.note: The worst park trip of the eighteen: M from Fresh Pond or Forest Av to 47-50 Sts-Rockefeller, then a B/D hop or ten-minute walk to the 59 St corner — locals lean on Forest Park and Highland Park instead.

crown-heights.note: One-seat but long: the 3 from Nostrand or Kingston runs to 72 St-Broadway with a two-avenue walk left over — though with Prospect Park and the Botanic Garden at your door, Central Park becomes a museum-day add-on.

prospect-heights.note: The weekday B from Seventh Av runs straight up Central Park West to the 72 St and 81 St entrances; the Q to 57 St-7 Av covers nights and weekends — but Prospect Park two blocks away answers most green-space needs first.

fort-greene.note: Atlantic Av-Barclays is the cheat code: B/D run one-seat up Central Park West and the Q reaches 57 St-7 Av; from Clinton Hill's C/G stops, add a transfer and about ten more minutes.

bed-stuy.note: The A express from Nostrand or Utica runs one-seat to 59 St-Columbus Circle at the park's southwest corner in ~35-40 minutes; from the G-train side of the neighborhood it's a transfer and closer to an hour.

chelsea.note: C/E from 23 St run direct to 59 St-Columbus Circle at the park's southwest corner, and the 1 does the same up Seventh; the 2/3 express from 14 St reaches 72 St-Broadway with a crosstown walk.

west-village.note: The A express from W 4 St hits 59 St-Columbus Circle in three stops — call it 15 minutes to the park's corner; the local C/E or the 1 from Christopher St run closer to 25.

east-village.note: N/R/W to Herald Sq leaves a seven-minute walk with your bag; the L to 14 St with an A/C/E or 1/2/3 transfer drops you inside Penn instead.

lower-east-side.note: F or M direct to Herald Sq then a seven-minute walk; with luggage, F to W 4 St plus an A/C/E up to Penn keeps the whole trip underground.

williamsburg.note: M to Herald Sq plus a short walk is the default; from the Bedford end, the L to 14 St connects to the A/C/E, which lands inside the station.

east-williamsburg.note: L to 14 St then A/C/E or 1/2/3 into Penn; near the Flushing Av edge the M reaches Herald Sq direct with the walk left over.

greenpoint.note: G to Court Sq then the E runs straight into Penn — the transfer is unavoidable, but at least you surface inside the station.

bushwick.note: M to Herald Sq leaves a seven-minute walk; from the Wilson Av end budget closer to an hour, which stings when you're racing an Amtrak departure.

astoria.note: N/W direct to Herald Sq then a seven-minute walk to Penn — no transfer, just remember to pad the walk when you have a train to catch.

long-island-city.note: E from Court Sq surfaces inside Penn a few stops later — the easiest train-escape setup of the ten; the 7 to Times Sq is the backup.

hells-kitchen.note: Most of Hell's Kitchen walks to Penn in 10-15 minutes; with luggage it's one A/C/E stop from 42 St or the 1 from 50 St.

yorkville.note: Q direct to Herald Sq then a seven-minute walk; transferring to the 1/2/3 at Times Sq lands you inside Penn, worth it with a suitcase.

harlem.note: 2/3 express from 125 St-Lenox lands inside Penn in about 15 minutes — one of the best train-escape setups in the city; the A from 125 St-St Nicholas does the same down the west side.

ridgewood.note: M from Fresh Pond or Seneca runs direct to Herald Sq, leaving the seven-minute walk with your bag; from the Myrtle-Wyckoff end the L + 14 St transfer to the A/C/E lands inside the station instead.

crown-heights.note: The 2/3 from Nostrand or Kingston run one-seat into Penn in roughly 30 minutes — no transfer, no street walk; the 4/5 across the platform are the faster play when it's Metro-North from Grand Central instead.

prospect-heights.note: 2/3 from Grand Army Plaza or Bergen St run one-seat into Penn in about 25 minutes; the B/Q from Seventh Av reach Herald Sq with the seven-minute walk left over.

fort-greene.note: From Atlantic Av-Barclays the 2/3 run one-seat into Penn in about 20 minutes, with N/R to Herald Sq as backup; from Clinton Hill the C from Lafayette Av also lands inside the station, just slower.

bed-stuy.note: The A express from Nostrand or Utica lands inside Penn in under 30 minutes, the local C closer to 40; from the G-corridor blocks budget extra for the walk to Fulton St or a transfer at Hoyt-Schermerhorn.

chelsea.note: From most of Chelsea you just walk — Penn is 10-15 minutes up Seventh or Eighth; with luggage it's one stop on the 1/2/3 or A/C/E from 23 St straight into the station.

west-village.note: The 2/3 express from 14 St reaches Penn in one stop and the A/C/E from W 4 St in two; even from Christopher St with a suitcase the whole trip stays under 20 minutes.
